nodes.body: Removing the `FloatingSubNav` export from the root entry changes the public import path (`@littlebranches/giselle-mui` → `@littlebranches/giselle-mui/motion`) and will break existing consumer imports. Please ensure this PR is treated as a breaking change in release notes/versioning (or provide an explicit migration note in the docs).

✅ Valid. The PR description acknowledged the breaking change but had no in-docs migration note. Fixed at commit `948cde2`: added a '## Breaking change — PR #67 (21 May 2026)' section to the FloatingSubNav README with the before/after import paths so consumers have a permanent, discoverable record alongside the component.

This sentence states the package already supports MUI v9, but `package.json` currently pins `@mui/material` peer/dev deps to `^7.0.0` (no dual-major window). Please reword to reflect the current published state (v7-only) or explicitly mark v9 support as planned/pending PR #62, so the roadmap doesn’t mislead consumers.

✅ Valid. The roadmap.mdx 'Current state' section was prematurely claiming v7+v9 support while `package.json` still pins `^7.0.0` and PR #62 is not yet merged. Fixed at commit `948cde2`: rewrote the sentence to 'currently supports **MUI v7** (`peerDependencies: "^7.0.0"`). Dual-major support (v7 + v9) is in progress — see PR #62.'
